text_fr: Considérant que le  Gouvernement de l'Union Sud-Africaine a, par une lettre du 23 juillet 194 7', informé l'Organisation des Nations Unies de sa dé› cision  de  ne  pas  procéder a l'incorporation du Sud-Ouest Africain dans I'Union Sud-Africaine, mais de maintenir le statu quo et de  continuer a administrer le Territoirc dans !'esprit du mandat actuellement en vigueur, et que le Gouvemement de l'Union Sud-Africaine s'est engagé a présenter des  rapports  pour  information a l'Organisation des Nations Unies;
text_en: Considering that the Government of the South African Union, by letter of 23 July 194, 7' has informed the United Nations of its decision not to incorporate South-West Africa into the South African Union, but to maintain the status quo and to continue to administer the Territory in the spirit of the current mandate, and that the South African Union's Government has undertaken to submit reports for information to the United Nations;

text_fr: Prend acte de la décision du Gouvemement de l'Union Sud-Africaine de ne pas procéder a l'in› corporation du Sud-Ouest Africain;

text_fr: M aintient fermement  sa  recommandation  de placer  le  Sud-Ouest Africain sous  le  Régime de tutellc;
text_en: Strongly supported his recommendation to place South West Africa under the Tutellc regime;

text_fr: Prie instamment le Gouvemement de !'Union Sud-Africaine  de  soumettre a !'examen de l'As› semblée générale un accord de tutelle pour le ter› ritoire du Sud-Ouest Africain et exprime l'espoir qu'il sera  possible  au Gouvernement de  l'Union de le faire en temps voulu, de maniere a permettre a I'Assemblée générale d'examiner cet accord lors de sa troisieme session.
text_en: Urges the South African Union's Government to submit to the General Assembly a tutelage agreement for the South West African Ritoire and expresses the hope that it will be possible for the Union Government to do so in a timely manner, so as to enable the General Assembly to consider this agreement at its third session.

items_en: l. That, with a view to presenting in the best possible way the summaries and analyses of the meetings transmitted pursuant to Article 73 e of the Charter, the Secretary-General be admitted to use, indicating the sources, the official publications of Members responsible for the administration of the Non-Self-Governing Territories, without prejudice to any information transmitted by them in application of the provisions of this Article;

2. That the use by the Secretary-General of the material derived from official publications referred to in paragraph 1 above be limited to the subjects covered in the information required by Article 73 e;
3. Only publications which may be transmitted or notified to the Secretary-General by the Member or Members who are responsible for the administration shall be used;
4. That, for the same purpose, the Secretary-General be permitted to use documents issued by intergovernmental or scientific bodies.
